back_translation: Then Job answered:
'Truly you are the people, and wisdom will die with you!
But I too have understanding like you; I am not inferior to you. Who does not know such things as these?
I have become a laughingstock to my friends, though I called on God and He answered. The righteous and blameless man is mocked.
The one at ease scorns misfortune, as the fate of those whose feet are slipping.
The tents of robbers are at peace, and those who provoke God are secure—those who carry their own god in their hands.
But ask the animals now, and they will teach you; ask the birds of the air, and they will tell you.
Or speak to the earth, and it will teach you; and the fish of the sea will declare to you.
Among all these, which does not know that the hand of the LORD has done this?
In His hand is the life of every living thing, and the breath of all mankind.
Does not the ear test words, as the tongue tastes its food?
Wisdom is with the aged, and understanding comes with length of days.
Wisdom and might belong to God; counsel and understanding are His.
Behold, what He tears down cannot be rebuilt; the one He shuts in cannot be released.
Behold, if He holds back the waters, they dry up; if He releases them, they overwhelm the land.
Strength and sound wisdom belong to Him; the deceived and the deceiver are His.
He leads counselors away stripped, and makes fools of judges.
He loosens the bonds that kings impose, and fastens a belt around their waists.
He leads priests away stripped, and overthrows the mighty.
He silences the trusted counselors, and takes away the discernment of elders.
He pours contempt on nobles, and disarms the powerful.
He reveals deep things out of darkness, and brings the shadow of death out into light.
He makes nations great and destroys them; He enlarges nations and scatters them.
He takes away the understanding of the leaders of the earth, and makes them wander in a trackless wasteland.
They grope in darkness without light, and He makes them stagger like a drunkard.'
